source: Smelling Salts Review — extracted from conversation history line 81 (GitHub Innovation Master List)
name: Maven Data Cooperative
description: Without the Maven Data Cooperative, Maven is just another platform extracting value from the poor. Ruby Red's data — her spending patterns, her struggles, her neighborhood needs — is currently harvested by banks, phone companies, and tech platforms for their benefit. The Maven Data Cooperative flips this: Ruby's data is anonymized, aggregated, and deployed for the village's collective benefit. Ruby owns her data. The village benefits from the aggregate.
north_star_alignment: The greedy capitalist world extracts data from Ruby Red and uses it against her (targeted predatory lending ads, dynamic pricing, credit score manipulation). The Maven Data Cooperative is the antidote — it uses the same data FOR her, collectively.
core_principles: Ruby Red owns her data — always. She can see it, export it, delete it at any time.
Individual data is NEVER shared, sold, or exposed. Only anonymized aggregates are used.
The cooperative exists to benefit the village, not Maven's bottom line.
Data sovereignty is a right, not a feature. It cannot be removed or downgraded by tier.
Transparency is absolute — Ruby can see exactly how her anonymized data is being used.
data_sources: Spending patterns (from Budget Builder and Vampire Slayer)
Bill payment history (from Fee Fighter and Bill Tracker)
Phone usage data (from Cooperative Phone Negotiation)
Neighborhood needs (from Grace conversations and Big Mama network)
Barter activity (from Good Neighbor Barter system)
Service usage patterns (from all 143 Maven services)

collective_use_cases.description: Aggregated data proves to phone companies, utilities, and insurers that Maven members represent a valuable collective. This data is the leverage for negotiating group rates.
collective_use_cases.example: 1,000 members paying an average of $95/month for phone = $95,000/month in collective spending power. That gets a carrier's attention.

collective_use_cases.description: Anonymized data reveals neighborhood-level needs — which blocks need food assistance, which areas have the highest NSF fee rates, where the payday lenders are clustering.
collective_use_cases.example: Data shows 40% of Ruby Reds in Mill Woods are paying NSF fees in the last week of the month. Maven can pre-position Milk Money offers in that window.

collective_use_cases.description: Aggregated data becomes evidence for policy change. When Maven can prove that 10,000 families are paying $500/year in hidden bank fees, that's a policy brief that gets attention.
collective_use_cases.example: The Puppet Strings Report uses cooperative data to show Ruby exactly what's happening behind the scenes — and the same data, aggregated, becomes a weapon for systemic change.

collective_use_cases.description: Patterns in the aggregate data allow Grace to predict when Ruby is about to hit a crisis — and intervene before it happens.
collective_use_cases.example: Grace sees that Ruby's spending pattern matches the pattern of 200 other members who hit an NSF fee 3 days later. Grace nudges Ruby now, not after the fee hits.

connection_to_puppet_strings_report.description: The Puppet Strings Report is the individual-facing transparency tool. It shows Ruby exactly what's happening behind the scenes in her financial life — who is charging her what, where the hidden fees are, what her data is being used for by other companies. The Data Cooperative is the collective version — using the same transparency to benefit the village.

governance.data_board: Elected from Maven members, reviews all data use quarterly
governance.transparency_report: Published monthly — what data was used, how, and what benefit it generated
governance.opt_out: Any member can opt out of the cooperative at any time without losing Maven membership

build_complexity: VERY HIGH — requires data infrastructure, legal framework, privacy compliance, governance structure
